mnemonics: Think of a DVD player seeking a scene (RANDOM ACCESS), not a cassette tape rewinding (SEQUENTIAL). The 'RandomAccessFile' class is your DVD remote with 'seek()' buttons.
keyPoints: It allows reading from and writing to any arbitrary position within a file, not just sequentially from the beginning.
It is implemented by the 'RandomAccessFile' class, which is not part of the core InputStream/OutputStream hierarchy.
The 'seek(long pos)' method is crucial for moving the file pointer to a specific byte position for the next read or write operation.
commonMistakes: Forgetting that file positions are byte-oriented (e.g., a 'long' value at position 0 is 8 bytes, so the next value starts at position 8).
Using it for simple sequential reading/writing, which is less efficient than using BufferedInputStream/BufferedOutputStream.
Not handling the possibility of an IOException when the specified position for seek() is beyond the end of the file.
